05-REQ-004.note: Persistent user record per human identity that has signed in, capturing at minimum canonical email, display name, and first-authentication timestamp. Identity semantics (email canonical) are cited from identity-and-authorization/email-as-canonical-identity, not restated.

05-REQ-005.note: Record created at the moment of first successful sign-in; canonical email immutable on the record; a provider-side email change is a new identity with a fresh record. Fork semantics owned by identity-and-authorization/email-as-canonical-identity#email-fork-keeps-history (dedupe cluster with retired 03-REQ-008); this capability carries only the record-side consequence.

05-REQ-007.note: User records never deleted (including on loss of Google-account access) and never merged across distinct canonical emails; historical attribution stays anchored to the original record. The attribution-preservation half of retired 03-REQ-047 lives with replay-and-audit.

08-REQ-010a.note: Authenticated home view: current team memberships, recently visited rooms, and games currently being played involving the user's teams, each linking to its detailed view. Room and game-status semantics stay with their owning capabilities; the home view is a hub of links.

08-REQ-023a.note: Teams browser listing teams (name, colour, captain) with entries linking to team profiles. The legacy 'all Centaur Teams' yields to the later archive semantics: the default listing hides archived teams per team-management/archive-not-delete while their profiles stay reachable.

08-REQ-090.note: Player Profile for every user record — own profile via global navigation, others linked at minimum from member listings and game histories. The authenticated-only half (08-REVIEW-016) is authored once in accounts-and-profiles/no-public-surface.

08-REQ-091.note: Profile contents: display name, current and historical memberships, chronological game history under the historical-or-current membership rule (cited from replay-and-audit/team-game-history) — and never an email, even on self-view (the email half is owned by accounts-and-profiles/email-confidentiality).

08-REQ-091a.note: No user-facing query, view, or exported shape exposes any user's email to anyone, the user themselves included; email stored solely for identity matching and administrative operations. The legacy downstream constraint on the platform query layer is authored as behaviour here: shapes omit email at the data-contract boundary, and participation snapshots store none (constraint-mining promotion of the legacy design's projection and email-free-rosterSnapshot notes).

08-REQ-092.note: Player aggregate statistics (games played, win rate, average team score at minimum) computed from exactly the data populating the game-history listing, hence consistent with it.

08-REQ-093.note: Player-profile historical attribution resolves via participating-team snapshots, never current records — a historical game shows the team of the day even after team changes or archiving.

08-REQ-094.note: Team Profile for every Centaur Team (archived included), visible to every authenticated user; the authenticated-only half (08-REVIEW-016) is authored once in accounts-and-profiles/no-public-surface.

08-REQ-094a.note: Global leaderboard ranking teams by one of a closed criteria set: at minimum win rate (with a minimum-games qualifying threshold), total wins, and average score — authored as average normalised score, the cross-game comparable form (decision in the change's design.md).

08-REQ-094b.note: Criterion switching plus time-window filtering from a closed set (at minimum all time, last 30 days, last 7 days).

08-REQ-094c.note: Optional restriction of the ranking to games played within a single room; the ranking then considers only that subset.

08-REQ-094d.note: Each ranked entry links to that team's Team Profile view; carried in the leaderboard requirement's body.

08-REQ-094e.note: Archived teams remain in the default leaderboard under their archived identity; rankings resolve via participating-team snapshots and archiving never rewrites competitive history (08-REVIEW-017; archive semantics cited from team-management/archive-not-delete).

08-REQ-094f.note: Leaderboard exposes nothing to unauthenticated visitors; folded into the single authenticated-only surface rule (08-REVIEW-016), citing identity-and-authorization/authentication-required.

08-REQ-095.note: Team Profile contents: name, colour, captain, current members, server nomination with latest health status (record substance cited from team-management/team-record), and the team's full chronological game history visible to any authenticated user (consistent with replay-and-audit/finished-games-public).

08-REQ-096.note: Team aggregate statistics: games played, win rate, average score, and a head-to-head record against every team ever played, derived from the same data as the history listing.

08-REQ-097.note: Head-to-head opponent attribution resolves via participating-team snapshots and is stable under opponent archiving (08-REVIEW-017).

08-REQ-098.note: UI mirror folded as a scenario: the Team Profile exposes no mutating affordance over team state; mutation authority lives with team-management (the profile at most links onward to team-management/team-management-view).

08-REVIEW-016.note: Profiles and leaderboard are authenticated-only ('public' means any authenticated user, never the internet), and emails are never exposed by any user-facing query — not even to the user on their own profile; email stays stored solely for identity matching and administrative operations.

08-REVIEW-017.note: Deletion is not a thing on this platform: archived teams persist in the default leaderboard under their archived identity, and archiving never rewrites history, statistics, or rankings (upstream resolution carried by team-management/archive-not-delete).
